short_post: Climate change is happening more than 10 times faster than natural warming after the last ice age, according to a National Academies report. Most of the 1°C global warming since 1900 occurred in just the past 50 years. {{ARTICLE_URL}} #BasedOnScience
long_post: How fast is too fast? When it comes to climate change, the answer might surprise you. Current warming is happening more than 10 times faster than the natural warming that ended the last ice age, according to National Academies research. The numbers tell a stark story: atmospheric CO2 has jumped over 40% since the Industrial Revolution, with more than half that increase occurring since just 1970. Global temperatures have risen about 1°C since 1900, but most of this warming happened in the last five decades. This unprecedented speed makes it much harder for people and nature to adapt. Thousands of species are already changing where they live and when they reproduce to keep up. {{ARTICLE_URL}} 🌡️
